title: Remove option image upload fields from Add Question modal

requirements.text: In the Admin Panel 'Add Question' modal (frontend/src/components/admin/AddQuestionModal.tsx), remove all four option image file picker inputs (Option 1–4). The modal must retain only: the single question image file picker (with live preview thumbnail), the correct option selector (radio buttons for A, B, C, D), and the Submit button. On submit, call the addQuestion backend function passing the single question image base64 data URI and empty strings (or a placeholder) for the four option image data fields to maintain backend compatibility. Close the modal on success, show a success toast, and refresh the question gallery.

quotes: remove image upload feature of options only at the time of question creation
only remove options image upload feature and now i am upload question + options in single image

acceptanceCriteria: The Add Question modal shows exactly one image file picker for the question image only.
No file picker inputs labeled Option 1, Option 2, Option 3, or Option 4 are present in the modal.
The correct option selector (radio buttons A/B/C/D) remains in the modal.
The question image file picker still shows a live preview thumbnail when a file is selected.
Submitting the form calls addQuestion with the question image base64 data URI; option image data fields are passed as empty strings to preserve backend function signature compatibility.
On successful submission the modal closes, a success toast is shown, and the question gallery refreshes.
All other modal behaviour (submit button, validation, loading state) remains unchanged.

constraints: Do not change the backend addQuestion function signature or data model.
Do not remove or modify the correct option selector (A/B/C/D radio buttons) in the modal.
Do not alter the question image file picker, its preview thumbnail, or the submit/cancel logic.
Timer, question navigation, submission logic, QuestionDisplay, and Test Page review section must remain completely unchanged.
nonGoals: Changing the backend Question data model or addQuestion function.
Modifying the Question Gallery display in the Admin Panel.
Modifying the QuestionDisplay component or Test Page.
Adding new fields or changing the correct option selector.
